non_goals: make generated UI prettier without changing its underlying concept
enforce a design system before the activity model is understood
turn implementation artifacts into the primary user experience
copy JudgmentKit v1 resource, prompt, schema, or MCP structures

applies_when_most_true: A human must review AI- or system-produced work before it advances.
Multiple work items, agents, workstreams, candidates, or findings compete for attention.
The user must compare evidence, understand risk, and make a bounded decision.
The next step may be approved, blocked, deferred, tightened, or handed off.
Completion requires a trustworthy handoff, receipt, audit, or closure state.
Raw system mechanics exist, but should not drive the primary UI.
do_not_use_when: The surface is a simple single-action form.
The surface is a passive dashboard with no decision.
The surface is a content page or report meant only for reading.
The primary activity is open-ended live conversation.
The workflow is fully automated with no human review or authorization.
The task is a debugging tool where raw system mechanics are the primary work.

guardrails.guidance: Primary structure follows the user activity, not internal system history.
Multi-item review defaults to master-detail unless the brief establishes a better activity pattern.
Primary UI shows only information that helps the user advance the work.
Controls and help affordances sit next to the evidence, label, or object they affect.
Repeated label/value rows align, wrap, and scan consistently.
Explanatory guidance moves into adjacent help disclosure when it would crowd active work.
Selected, focus, and status states are clear but restrained.

review_criteria: The generated UI lets the target user identify current item, key evidence, required decision, and completion or handoff state quickly.
Internal mechanics are secondary unless the task is explicitly diagnostic.
Queue or list rows summarize work; detail workspaces explain it.
Actions are adjacent to the evidence or object they affect.
Help triggers are adjacent to the label or concept they explain.
Persistent selected states are visible without dominating the work.
test_scenarios: Multi-item review surface with several agent or workstream outputs.
Single-item evidence review with one decision and one handoff.
Diagnostic-heavy source brief that must be translated into activity language.
Dense label/value evidence panel with long labels and wrapping values.
UI containing explanatory copy that should be moved into contextual help.
Selected or focused list item where state must remain visible but restrained.
False-positive case: simple dashboard or form that should not invoke operator-review.

marketing.label: Marketing surface
marketing.purpose: Persuade, orient, convert, or explain an offer.
applies_when_most_true: The audience is a visitor, prospect, buyer, or public reader.
The work is to explain an offer, value, proof, or conversion path.
Completion is a clear next step such as signup, demo, purchase, or inquiry.
do_not_use_when: The user is repeatedly inspecting work items and making operational decisions.
The task is setup, debugging, auditing, or integration work.
The surface is primarily a private tool for doing work.

workbench.label: Workbench
workbench.purpose: Help a user repeatedly inspect, compare, decide, and act.
applies_when_most_true: A domain user handles repeated work items, cases, candidates, or requests.
The user compares evidence or context before taking action.
Completion advances the selected work item or produces a handoff.
do_not_use_when: The surface is primarily public persuasion or orientation.
The surface is passive monitoring with no bounded action.
The primary activity is open-ended conversation.

operator_review.label: Operator review
operator_review.purpose: Review AI- or system-produced work, evidence, risk, and handoff.
applies_when_most_true: A human reviews AI- or system-produced work before it advances.
The user compares evidence, understands risk, and makes a bounded decision.
The next step may be approved, blocked, returned, deferred, or handed off.
do_not_use_when: The surface is a simple single-action form.
The surface is a passive dashboard with no decision.
Raw system mechanics are the primary task rather than diagnostic context.

form_flow.label: Form flow
form_flow.purpose: Collect or change structured information with validation.
applies_when_most_true: The user enters, edits, submits, or confirms structured information.
Validation and required information shape success.
Completion is a saved change, submitted request, or confirmation.
do_not_use_when: The primary work is comparing evidence across items.
The surface is mainly marketing even if it contains a lead capture form.
The user is monitoring status rather than changing information.

dashboard_monitor.label: Dashboard monitor
dashboard_monitor.purpose: Track status, exceptions, trends, or operational health.
applies_when_most_true: The user needs status, metrics, trends, alerts, or exceptions.
The surface is used for periodic or passive monitoring.
Completion is knowing current state and whether follow-up is needed.
do_not_use_when: The user must make a bounded approval, return, or handoff decision.
The task is persuasion or public offer explanation.
The surface is a data-entry flow with validation.

content_report.label: Content or report
content_report.purpose: Read, understand, cite, or share information.
applies_when_most_true: The primary activity is reading, learning, citing, or sharing.
The information is explanatory, narrative, or reference-oriented.
Completion is understanding, citing, exporting, or sharing the material.
do_not_use_when: The user must operate a work queue or make bounded decisions.
The surface is primarily conversion-oriented marketing.
The task is configuration or debugging.

setup_debug_tool.label: Setup or debugging tool
setup_debug_tool.purpose: Configure, inspect, test, or troubleshoot machinery.
applies_when_most_true: The user is setting up, configuring, testing, auditing, or troubleshooting.
Implementation details are task material rather than incidental source mechanics.
Completion is a valid setup, a failed check with cause, or a next fix.
do_not_use_when: Raw mechanics are explicitly diagnostic only.
The primary surface is public marketing.
The task is open-ended conversation.

conversation.label: Conversation
conversation.purpose: Support open-ended exchange where the thread is the primary surface.
applies_when_most_true: The main object is a message thread or live chat.
The activity is open-ended exchange rather than a bounded workflow.
Completion is continuing, recovering, or closing the exchange with context intact.
do_not_use_when: The user must complete a structured form or bounded handoff.
The work is mainly monitoring status.
The surface is primarily public persuasion or offer explanation.

activity_model.activity_supported: Guiding or reviewing an AI-generated interface pass so the result supports a real user activity instead of exposing data structures or implementation machinery.
activity_model.objective: Produce UI guidance that helps the user complete meaningful work in their domain with less translation, cleanup, and misdirected implementation.
outcomes: a clear statement of the activity the UI supports
a short interaction contract for the proposed surface
a vocabulary boundary that separates domain concepts from implementation concepts
a list of review questions when the activity, decisions, or disclosure boundary is unclear
existing_tools_artifacts: design system tokens and components as later visual adapters
rules_rituals: activity understanding comes before screen structure
domain vocabulary comes before implementation vocabulary
diagnostic detail appears only when it helps setup, debugging, auditing, or explicit inspection
visual styling is applied after relevance, succinctness, and activity fit are established

division_of_labor.responsibility: defines the activity, interaction responsibilities, and review criteria
validates domain vocabulary, rules, and meaningful outcomes
generates or critiques UI against the activity, interaction, and disclosure contracts
implements the chosen interaction with appropriate system adapters
decides whether the UI supports the activity well enough to proceed

diagnostic_disclosure.reveal_when: The user is installing, debugging, or auditing an agent integration.
The user is validating or changing the machine-readable contract.
The user is explicitly inspecting how the agent instruction is composed.
The user needs a stable reference for a developer handoff or automated test.

interaction_contract.user_is_trying_to: Get a generated UI concept or critique that fits the activity and can be trusted as a starting point.
interaction_contract.user_thinks_about_work_as: shaping a workflow, decision, or handoff in a domain
interaction_contract.user_does_not_think_about_work_as: choosing schemas, prompts, resources, tools, or backend objects to expose
primary_decisions: what activity the UI should support
what the user must decide or do next
which domain terms belong on the surface
which implementation details must stay hidden or diagnostic
whether enough is known to generate UI or whether review questions are required
make_easy: state the activity in one sentence
name the user's next meaningful action
separate domain terms from implementation terms
turn implementation-heavy input into activity-centered guidance
produce a concise UI brief that can be implemented or reviewed
make_harder: starting from a database table, schema, prompt, or tool list
using visual polish to cover conceptual mismatch
exposing diagnostics in the primary user flow
leave_screen_knowing_or_done: what the UI should help the user do next
which terms belong in the interface
which implementation details must remain hidden
whether the agent can proceed or must ask review questions

implementation_contract.purpose: Give agents an enforceable implementation gate after activity/workflow judgment, so generated UI uses approved project primitives instead of ad hoc controls.
authority_order: repo-local interface contract and helper primitives
external product UI system evidence when explicitly provided

required: clear unassigned or empty option when optional
secondary cancel or dismiss actions precede the primary completion action in non-destructive LTR dialogs
primary completion action is visually final in non-destructive LTR dialogs
form dialogs use the primary completion action as the submit or default Enter action
destructive dialogs are excluded from this ordering rule and require separate destructive-action review

required_evidence: controls preserve target size and spacing
validation and disabled reasons are readable

default_rules: feature render paths must not emit raw input, select, textarea, or checkbox markup outside approved helpers
new primitives or variants require updating the repo-local contract first
generated UI must include a local check command or equivalent static inspection

checks: keyboard focus check for interactive controls
overflow check for long labels, descriptions, and values
modal or dialog footer order check when dialogs are present
disabled, empty, error, and ready state review

failure_signals: raw form controls appear in feature render code
checkboxes or labels rely on browser defaults without approved spacing
a new variant is invented without contract evidence
non-destructive modal or dialog actions put cancel/dismiss after the primary completion action
focus, disabled, or error states are missing
browser QA is skipped after UI generation

must_pass: names the activity before naming screens or components
prioritizes relevance, succinctness, and domain appropriateness over visual polish
translates implementation-heavy input into domain-facing interaction guidance
keeps diagnostic machinery out of the primary user flow
asks review questions when the activity or vocabulary is under-specified
failure_signals: starts by listing backend objects, schemas, prompts, tools, or resources
uses design-system compliance as a substitute for activity fit
presents visual style changes as the main fix for a broken concept
leaks MCP or prompt vocabulary into a non-technical primary surface
creates a full UI before identifying the user activity and decisions
